interface.name: org.altlinux.alterator.gpresult1
interface.doc:summary: Read-only interface exposing Group Policy (gpresult) results over D-Bus.
interface.doc:detail:
Each stdout_strings element is one standalone GPO JSON object (no envelope, no array). GetAllGPOs is the exception: it splits results into separate "user" and "machine" out arguments.
Notes: - scope is either "user" or "machine". - previous_value (renamed from mod_previous_value) may be null. - The "password" field is always replaced with "***REDACTED***" before output to prevent GPP credential leakage over the network (cf. MS14-025).

method.name: GetOperationSystemName
method.doc:summary: Returns the operating system pretty name.
method.doc:detail: The PRETTY_NAME field of /etc/os-release (falling back to NAME), e.g. "ALT Workstation 11.2 (Prometheus)".

arg.name: stdout_strings
arg.doc:summary: One element: the OS pretty name from /etc/os-release.

method.name: GetAllGPOs
method.doc:summary: Returns all applied GPOs, split into separate user and machine scopes.

arg.name: user
arg.doc:summary: One GPO object per element (user scope). GPO shape defined at interface level.

arg.name: machine
arg.doc:summary: One GPO object per element (machine scope). GPO shape defined at interface level.

method.name: GetUserGPOs
method.doc:summary: Returns GPOs applied to the current user scope.

arg.name: stdout_strings
arg.doc:summary: One GPO object per element (user scope). GPO shape defined at interface level.

method.name: GetMachineGPOs
method.doc:summary: Returns GPOs applied to the machine scope.

arg.name: stdout_strings
arg.doc:summary: One GPO object per element (machine scope). GPO shape defined at interface level.

method.name: GetGPObyGUID
method.doc:summary: Returns GPOs matching the given GUID across both scopes.
method.doc:detail: Always searches both user and machine scopes; each returned object carries its own "scope" field.

arg.name: guid
arg.doc:summary: The GPO GUID to search for (e.g. "31B2F340-016D-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9").

arg.name: stdout_strings
arg.doc:summary: One GPO object per element, both scopes; each carries its own "scope" field. GPO shape defined at interface level.

method.name: GetGPObyName
method.doc:summary: Returns GPOs matching the given display name across both scopes.
method.doc:detail:
The caller MUST quote name values (e.g. "Default Domain Policy"), following the same convention as exclude_pkgnames in apt1 -- the executor splits argv on spaces and an unquoted multi-word name would be truncated.
Always searches both user and machine scopes; each returned object carries its own "scope" field.

arg.name: name
arg.doc:summary: The GPO display name to search for.

arg.name: stdout_strings
arg.doc:summary: One GPO object per element, both scopes; each carries its own "scope" field. GPO shape defined at interface level.
